research: RaBitQ unbiased estimator (Gao & Long, SIGMOD 2024). Implemented faithfully: estimate <q,o> = (2*sum_{set bits} q'_i - sum q') / ||o'||_1, q' = rotated query, ||o'||_1 stored per vector. The per-vector L1 factor unbiases vs plain asymmetric.
note: RaBitQ estimator gives FAR better recall-per-bit than symmetric Hamming: stage-1 0.606 vs rot-sym 0.463 (+14pts); reaches 0.985 @ C=100 where symmetric needs C~500-1000 -> ~5-10x smaller C. BUT scan is 40x slower (14.7 vs ~580 QPS) - it's the asymmetric set-bit-gather kernel (no popcount/LUT), same speed issue as 010/011. Recall-best, needs the fast bit-quantized/vpshufb estimator to be deployable (parked).
